snippets.title: Module-level logger pattern
snippets.code:
import logging

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


def chunk_text(text: str, max_tokens: int) -> list[str]:
    logger.debug("chunking text len=%d max_tokens=%d", len(text), max_tokens)
    if not text:
        logger.info("empty input, returning []")
        return []
    chunks = _do_chunk(text, max_tokens)
    logger.info("chunked into %d chunks", len(chunks))
    return chunks
snippets.explanation: `getLogger(__name__)` is the universal Python idiom — names follow the package path, config cascades naturally.

snippets.title: Logging exceptions properly
snippets.explanation: `.exception` from inside `except` is the cleanest pattern; `exc_info=True` works elsewhere.

snippets.title: App-level config (`dictConfig`)
snippets.explanation: Configure once at app start. Loggers can have per-module overrides; rotating file handler keeps disk usage bounded.

snippets.title: Structured logging with structlog
snippets.code:
# pip install structlog python-json-logger
import structlog, logging, sys

structlog.configure(
    processors=[
        structlog.contextvars.merge_contextvars,
        structlog.processors.add_log_level,
        structlog.processors.TimeStamper(fmt="iso"),
        structlog.processors.JSONRenderer(),
    ],
    wrapper_class=structlog.make_filtering_bound_logger(logging.INFO),
    logger_factory=structlog.PrintLoggerFactory(file=sys.stderr),
)

log = structlog.get_logger("ragapp")

from structlog.contextvars import bind_contextvars, clear_contextvars

def handle_request(request_id: str, user_id: str):
    clear_contextvars()
    bind_contextvars(request_id=request_id, user_id=user_id)

    log.info("request received")
    # ... downstream calls automatically inherit request_id, user_id ...
    log.warning("rate limited", attempt=3, delay_s=8)
snippets.explanation: `bind_contextvars` puts request_id/user_id on every subsequent log line on this task — magic for correlation.

snippets.title: Don't configure logging in libraries
snippets.code:
# library: src/ragapp/retrieval/__init__.py
import logging
logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)         # ← ONLY this

# NEVER do this in a library:
# logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO)    # WRONG — fights the app's config

# Library users add `logging.NullHandler()` to prevent 'no handler' warnings:
logger.addHandler(logging.NullHandler())
snippets.explanation: Libraries name their logger and stop there; the *application* owns format, level, destination. `NullHandler` avoids the noisy 'no handler' message if no app config is loaded.
